nodes.name: Ahimsa
nodes.description: Non-harming; an ethical orientation of restraint from violence and injury.

nodes.name: Anatta
nodes.description: Not-self; the absence of a permanent, independent self in conditioned phenomena.

nodes.name: Anicca
nodes.description: Impermanence; the conditioned nature of phenomena as changing and unstable.

nodes.name: Appamada
nodes.description: Heedfulness or diligent care in practice.

nodes.name: Avalokitesvara
nodes.description: A bodhisattva associated with compassion and prominent in the Heart Sutra setting.

nodes.name: Avidya
nodes.description: Ignorance or misknowing, especially regarding the nature of reality and the path.

nodes.name: Bodhicitta
nodes.description: The awakened mind or aspiration to attain awakening for the benefit of all beings.

nodes.name: Bodhisattva
nodes.description: A being oriented toward awakening, especially for the liberation of all beings in Mahayana contexts.

nodes.name: Brahmaviharas
nodes.description: The four divine abodes: loving-kindness, compassion, sympathetic joy, and equanimity.

nodes.name: Citta
nodes.description: Mind, heart, or mental orientation; a key term for understanding intention and experience.

nodes.name: Dukkha
nodes.description: A central Dharma concept often translated as suffering, unsatisfactoriness, or stress.

nodes.name: Elements
nodes.description: Elements or domains used to analyze experience and phenomena.

nodes.name: Five Aggregates
nodes.description: Form, feeling, perception, formations, and consciousness as a framework for analyzing experience.

nodes.name: Form
nodes.description: Material form; one of the five aggregates.

nodes.name: Four Noble Truths
nodes.description: The teaching of suffering, its origin, its cessation, and the path leading to cessation.

nodes.name: Karma
nodes.description: Intentional action and its ethical consequences.

nodes.name: Karuna
nodes.description: Compassion; the wish for beings to be free from suffering.

nodes.name: Klesha
nodes.description: Afflictive mental states that disturb the mind and condition suffering.

nodes.name: Mantra
nodes.description: A sacred utterance or formula used in contemplative and ritual contexts.

nodes.name: Metta
nodes.description: Loving-kindness; the cultivation of goodwill and friendliness.

nodes.name: Middle Way
nodes.description: A path avoiding extremes, often framed as avoiding indulgence and self-mortification or eternalism and nihilism.

nodes.name: Mudita
nodes.description: Sympathetic joy; delight in the welfare and happiness of others.

nodes.name: Nirvana
nodes.description: Liberation; the extinguishing of greed, hatred, and delusion.

nodes.name: Noble Eightfold Path
nodes.description: The path of right view, intention, speech, action, livelihood, effort, mindfulness, and concentration.

nodes.name: Paramita
nodes.description: Perfection or transcendent virtue cultivated on the bodhisattva path.

nodes.name: Prajna
nodes.description: Wisdom or liberating insight into the nature of phenomena.

nodes.name: Prajnaparamita
nodes.description: The perfection of wisdom; a central theme in Mahayana sutra literature.

nodes.name: Pratityasamutpada
nodes.description: Dependent arising; the principle that phenomena arise in dependence on causes and conditions.

nodes.name: Samadhi
nodes.description: Collectedness, concentration, or meditative absorption.

nodes.name: Samsara
nodes.description: The cycle of birth, death, and rebirth conditioned by ignorance and craving.

nodes.name: Sila
nodes.description: Ethical conduct or moral discipline.

nodes.name: Six Sense Bases
nodes.description: The internal and external sense bases involved in perceptual experience.

nodes.name: Sunyata
nodes.description: A Mahayana concept commonly translated as emptiness.

nodes.name: Tanha
nodes.description: Craving or thirst; a central condition in the arising of suffering.

nodes.name: Three Marks of Existence
nodes.description: The marks of impermanence, suffering or unsatisfactoriness, and not-self.

nodes.name: Two Truths
nodes.description: The distinction between conventional truth and ultimate truth.

nodes.name: Upekkha
nodes.description: Equanimity; balanced presence toward changing experience.

nodes.name: Giac Khang Pilot Corpus
nodes.description: A bounded pilot corpus for testing evidence-first Dharma Knowledge Graph ingestion.

nodes.name: Giac Khang MVP Notes
nodes.description: A single pilot document used to test document-to-evidence modeling.

nodes.name: Evidence: Dependent arising as relational explanation
nodes.evidence_text: Dependent arising is represented as the principle that phenomena appear through conditions and relationships.

nodes.name: Evidence: Middle Way avoids extremes
nodes.evidence_text: The middle way is represented as avoiding fixed extremes while preserving practical conventional meaning.

nodes.name: Evidence: Sunyata as non-independent existence
nodes.evidence_text: Emptiness is represented as the absence of independent, self-existing nature rather than simple nothingness.

nodes.name: Unreviewed Giac Khang transcript evidence sample
nodes.evidence_text: Placeholder transcript excerpt for DKG-002 schema validation. This text is not reviewed and must be replaced with a real transcript excerpt before use.
nodes.speaker: Giac Khang
nodes.notes: Unreviewed placeholder node for DKG-002. Do not treat as verified teaching evidence.

nodes.name: Nagarjuna
nodes.description: A major Buddhist philosopher associated with Madhyamaka.

nodes.name: Bodh Gaya
nodes.description: A major Buddhist pilgrimage place associated with the Buddha's awakening.

nodes.name: Gandhara
nodes.description: A historical Buddhist region important for textual and artistic transmission.

nodes.name: Magadha
nodes.description: An ancient region important in early Buddhist history.

nodes.name: Nalanda
nodes.description: A historic center of Buddhist monastic learning.

nodes.name: Sarnath
nodes.description: A major Buddhist pilgrimage place associated with the first teaching.

nodes.name: Sri Lanka
nodes.description: A major historical center for Theravada Buddhist transmission.

nodes.name: Early Buddhism
nodes.description: A working category for early Buddhist teachings and textual layers.

nodes.name: Madhyamaka
nodes.description: A Mahayana philosophical school associated with analysis of emptiness.

nodes.name: Mahayana
nodes.description: A broad family of Buddhist traditions emphasizing the bodhisattva path.

nodes.name: Pali Canon
nodes.description: A textual tradition preserving canonical materials in Pali.

nodes.name: Theravada
nodes.description: A Buddhist tradition preserving the Pali Canon.

nodes.name: Giac Khang Study Notes
nodes.description: Pilot source material used to model evidence before API, embeddings, or LLM extraction.
